prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on benzene (12 atoms: 6 C + 6 H) using DFT at the tier 'standard' level — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted) — in PySCF. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry taken from the user-supplied benzene.xyz file; single-point (not re-optimized at this level). SCF converged in 8 cycles.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference, not an excitation energy).
HOMO (index 20): −7.0468329 eV
LUMO (index 21): −0.41574728 eV

The HOMO/HOMO−1 pair (−7.0468/−7.0473 eV) and LUMO/LUMO+1 pair (−0.41575/−0.41513 eV) are near-degenerate, consistent with benzene's D₆h symmetry (e₁g HOMO and e₂u LUMO pairs).

Integrity: trustworthy = true; all engine checks passed. Warnings array: empty — no warnings reported.
